B1528 SIROCCO · Issue #2760

B1528 SIROCCO current/rudder force review — Issue #2760

1. Introduction

The B1528 SIROCCO is a moored vessel. When current flows past it, two separate loads act on the ship:

  1. A hull current load — the current pushes on the hull, producing a longitudinal force (X), a transverse force (Y), and a yaw moment (N) about the centre of gravity.
  2. A rudder-induced load — current also flows past the rudder. When the rudder is held at an angle, it generates an additional force at the stern that contributes to X, Y, and N.

This report estimates both load components for the approved review case:

  • Current speed: 3.08 knots
  • Current heading ψ: +5° off the bow (port positive)
  • Rudder angle δ: +28° (port)
  • Propeller: stopped (rpm = 0; rotation factor Cr = 1.0)

All forces are reported in ship-fixed axes about the centre of gravity. This is a screening calculation intended to support mooring reaction-load reviews — it is not a certified hydrodynamic model. Limitations are in §6.

Default speed policy: 3.08 kn is the issue #2760 default current speed; sensitivity plots span 0..5 kn (4 kn = upper engineering bound; 5 kn = sensitivity extreme).

2. Design Data & Assumptions

Values used by the calculation are sourced from the packaged B1528 SIROCCO YAML input pack.

LBP (length between perpendiculars)225.5 m
Beam32.26 m
Draft12.20 m
Water depth100.0 m
Water depth / Draft (WD/T)8.20
Centre of gravity (longitudinal datum)midship
Centre of gravity (vertical, above keel)6.1 m
Yaw lever arm (rudder force to CoG)135.30 m
Rudder area A_R44.94 m²
Rudder span9.00 m
Water density ρ1025.0 kg/m³
Whicker-Fehlner constant β (see [3] in References)600.0
Propeller rotation factor Cr1.0 (rpm = 0)
Current speed (default)3.08 kn
Current heading ψ (default)+5° (port positive)
Rudder angle δ (default)+28° (port positive)
Current speed sweep range0..5 kn (plotted bound, not the design value)
Heading sweep-5° .. +5° in 1° steps
Rudder sweep0° .. +28° port in 2° steps
Input sourceProject Document — B1528 SIROCCO vessel particulars source-pack ([P1] in References)

3. Axes & Sign Conventions

All forces and moments are reported in ship-fixed axes at the centre of gravity using the right-hand rule.

SymbolMeaningSign convention
+Xlongitudinal force (along ship centreline)forward
+Ytransverse force (lateral, across ship)port
+Nyaw moment about CoGbow-to-port
ψcurrent heading offset from bowport positive
δrudder angle relative to ship centrelineport positive
αeffective rudder inflow angle = δ - ψ(derived)
Figure 0 — Axes & Sign Conventions. Top-down plan view, ship-fixed CoG frame per OCIMF MEG4 [1] Annex A §A.1. Cardinal numerals at frame edges (0° stern, 180° bow, 90° starboard, 270° port) define heading convention; ψ = current heading offset from bow (port positive), δ = rudder angle (port positive), α = δ - ψ = effective rudder inflow angle. +F_X = longitudinal force component; +F_Y = transverse force component (positive port); +M_XY = yaw moment about CoG (positive bow-to-port, right-hand rule). The current-direction arrow rotates with the heading offset and the rudder blade rotates with the rudder angle in the interactive HTML; PDF/DOCX shows the schematic at the selected default-case angles.

4. Load Due to Current

The current load on the hull is estimated using generic-reference OCIMF MEG3/MEG4 loaded-tanker current coefficients interpolated from the licensed off-repo workbook. These curves are a tanker-class basis — they are not vessel-specific to SIROCCO.

Three coefficients drive the load: Cxc (longitudinal) from Annex A figure A9, Cyc (transverse) from A10, and Cxyc (yaw moment) from A11. Workbook bucket selection: loaded-tanker family, water-depth-to-draft ratio WD/T > 6 (vessel WD/T = 8.20). Coefficients are interpolated at the table angle 180° − |ψ| = 175° for the default ψ = +5°.

4.1. Force calculation

Dynamic pressure q = 0.5 × ρ × V²; frontal area A_f = beam × draft; lateral area A_l = LBP × draft. Force components: longitudinal Xc = q × A_f × Cxc; transverse Yc = q × A_l × Cyc.

Figure 1 — Sign Convention & Current Loading. Top-down plan view, B1528 SIROCCO at default case (ψ=+5°, V=3.08 kn). +F_X = longitudinal current force on hull (component of q·A_f·Cxc along ship centreline; sign per OCIMF MEG4 [1] Annex A Fig. A9, Cxc≈−0.0324 at table angle 175°). +F_Y = transverse current force on hull (q·A_l·Cyc across ship; positive port; OCIMF MEG4 [1] Annex A Fig. A10, Cyc≈+0.0341). Cardinal labels at frame edges (0° stern / 180° bow / 90° starboard / 270° port) define heading convention per OCIMF Annex A §A.1. ψ inset shows the current angle-of-attack relative to bow. CoG at midship; vertical CoG 6.1 m above keel.

Sample calculation at default values:

  • V = 3.08 kn × 0.51444 = 1.5845 m/s
  • q = 0.5 × 1025 × (1.5845)² = 1286.7 Pa
  • A_f = 32.26 × 12.20 = 393.57 m²
  • A_l = 225.5 × 12.20 = 2751.10 m²
  • Cxc(175°) ≈ −0.0324, Cyc(175°) ≈ +0.0341
  • Xc ≈ -16.4 kN (longitudinal current force)
  • Yc ≈ 120.7 kN (transverse current force)

4.2. Yaw moment about CoG

Transverse current force Yc input — Method B (force × lever arm) multiplies the transverse current force Yc by a center-of-pressure lever arm. The chart below shows Yc across the heading sweep -5° to +5° in 1° steps at the selected current speed (default 3.08 kn). This is the Yc input that drives Method B's yaw moment estimate.

Two methods estimate the current yaw moment, shown side by side as a sanity check:

  • Method A — OCIMF direct (default): Nc_A = q × A_l × LBP × Cxyc(175°)
  • Method B — force × lever arm: Nc_B = Yc × CoP_lever_arm

These two methods rest on different assumptions and may differ. Chart 4 below shows both as an overlay with an explicit non-equality caption.

Figure 2 — Yaw Moment about CoG. Two estimation methods for current yaw moment, shown side by side as a sanity check (NOT an equality test). Method A (default): M_XY,A = q·A_l·LBP·Cxyc(table_angle) per OCIMF MEG4 [1] Annex A Fig. A11 (loaded-tanker WD/T>6 family). Method B (comparison): M_XY,B = F_Y × CoP_arm where CoP_arm ≈ 0.3·LBP is an approximate center-of-pressure lever arm for loaded-tanker near-head current (OCIMF MEG4 [1] Annex A guidance, typical range 0.2–0.4·LBP). Methods rest on different assumptions and may differ — see §4.2.1 chart.

4.2.1. Side-by-side yaw moment chart — Method A vs Method B

The chart below overlays both methods across the current-heading sweep at the default rudder angle. Method A uses the OCIMF direct yaw moment coefficient Cxyc; Method B multiplies the OCIMF transverse force Yc by an approximate center-of-pressure lever arm (~0.3·LBP). These methods rest on different assumptions and may differ — this overlay is a sanity check, NOT an equality test.

4.3. Interactive: current vs rudder vs total side-by-side

Use the controls below to switch current speed and rudder angle and see how the OCIMF current component, the rudder component, and their sum combine.

5. Load Due to Rudder

The rudder-induced force comes from current flowing past the rudder blade at the effective inflow angle α = δ - ψ. At the default case (δ = +28° port, ψ = +5° port): α = +23°.

Two screening rudder models are presented (Pass C wires Model B numerically; this Pass A lays out the structure):

  • Model A — Whicker-Fehlner normal-force basis (current default): F = β × A_R × V² × Cr × sin(α). Source: B1528 SIROCCO source pack (β = 600, Cr = 1.0).
  • Model B — thin-plate drag/lift (Pass C): Cn(α) ≈ 2π·sin(α) for small angles, with stall handling at ~25–30°. Reference: Faltinsen, Sea Loads on Ships and Offshore Structures, 1990, §6.5.

Both models are screening-level; neither is a validated rudder hydrodynamic model. Differences at large angles (>20°) reflect stall-region behaviour.

Figure 3 — Rudder Loading. Top-down view with rudder at default deflection δ=+28° port, current at ψ=+5° off the bow. Effective rudder inflow angle α = δ - ψ = +23°. F = rudder normal force (perpendicular to blade); decomposed at rudder pivot into +F_X (longitudinal, along centreline) and +F_Y (transverse, port). Two models compute F: Model A (Whicker-Fehlner [3], F = β·A_R·V²·Cr·sin(α)) and Model B (Faltinsen [2] §6.5, F = 0.5·ρ·A_R·V²·2π·sin(α) with stall cap). Comparison in §5.6.

5.1. Sample calculation at default values (Whicker-Fehlner - Model A)

Representative hand-check point: issue #2760 default 3.08 kn, heading +5 deg, rudder +28 deg, Cr=1.0.

  1. Speed conversion: V = 3.08 kn × 0.51444 = 1.5845 m/s
  2. Effective rudder inflow angle: α = δ - ψ = 28° - 5° = 23°
  3. Whicker-Fehlner base force (Model A): F = β × A_R × V² × Cr = 600 × 44.94 × (1.5845)² × 1.0 ≈ 68 kN
  4. Rudder normal force: Fn = F × sin(α) ≈ 26 kN
  5. Rudder load resolved to ship-fixed axes at CoG: longitudinal X_rudder ≈ 8 kN, transverse Y_rudder ≈ 25 kN (+port), yaw moment N_rudder ≈ 3404 kN·m (+bow-to-port)
  6. Current load (OCIMF MEG4 [1] Annex A Fig. A9/A10/A11 interpolation at table angle 175°): X_current ≈ -17 kN (longitudinal), Y_current ≈ 121 kN (+port), N_current ≈ 6725 kN·m (+bow-to-port)
  7. Combined at CoG (sum of current + rudder components): X_total ≈ -8 kN, Y_total ≈ 146 kN, N_total ≈ 10128 kN·m — these sums are reported for engineering review only; not a validated whole-vessel force balance

5.2. Rudder force over heading

Shows rudder-induced longitudinal X and transverse Y in kN for the selected current speed and rudder angle. Default rudder angle: 28° port.

5.3. Rudder yaw moment over heading

Shows signed rudder-induced yaw moment N (kN·m, +bow-to-port) over heading offset and rudder angle for the selected current speed.

5.4. Selected-speed envelope summary

Key variables for this section: current speed V = 3.08 kn (default) · heading sweep ψ ∈ [-5°, +5°] at 1° steps · rudder sweep δ ∈ [0°, +28°] at 2° steps. Envelope is the absolute-max across the full heading × rudder grid at the selected speed.

Envelope values update with the current-speed selector and use the full heading × rudder grid at that speed. The selected default is 3.08 kn; 5 kn is the upper bound of the sensitivity range.

MetricEnvelope caseValue
Selected speedengineering sweep plane3.08 kn · 165 rows
Max |Y| at selected speedψ=-5° · δ=28°29 kN
Max |N| at selected speedψ=-5° · δ=28°3931 kN·m
Max |X| at selected speedψ=-5° · δ=28°23 kN

Initial values are at the default current speed (3.08 kn); the interactive HTML updates the row above when the speed selector changes.

5.5. Selected-case force breakdown

Key variables for this section: current speed V = 3.08 kn (default; user-selectable via dropdown above) · heading ψ = +5° · rudder δ = +28° · effective rudder inflow α = δ − ψ = +23°. Static rows below are at the default case; JS updates them when the user changes the speed or rudder selector.

Updates with current speed and rudder angle. Values shown at the heading with maximum absolute total yaw moment for the selected trace, so individual X/Y/N component paths can be reviewed together.

ComponentX (kN, longitudinal)Y (kN, transverse port)N (kN·m, yaw bow-to-port)
Current (OCIMF MEG4 [1])-171216725
Rudder (Whicker-Fehlner [3])8253404
Total (current + rudder) / mooring reaction = negated-814610128

Default case: V=3.08 kn · ψ=5° · δ=28° · total yaw reaction for mooring = -10128 kN·m.

5.6. Current-speed sensitivity (0..5 knots)

Key variables for this section: heading ψ = +5° (fixed) · rudder δ = +28° (fixed) · current speed V sweep 0..5 kn. The plots below show how the current load and rudder load scale with current speed at the default heading/rudder; the selected default case (V = 3.08 kn) is marked on each plot.

Sensitivity plots use the engineering sweep at fixed heading +5° and rudder +28°. Both current and rudder loads scale as V² (quadratic in current speed) per the underlying physics.

6. Limitations

  • Generic-reference OCIMF tanker-current coefficients are not vessel-specific to SIROCCO. The report basis is an off-class screening tier.
  • Both rudder models are screening-level; neither is a validated rudder hydrodynamic model.
  • Component sums (X_total = Xc + Xr, etc.) are reported for engineering review; they are not a validated whole-vessel force balance.
  • When rudder_angle_deg equals heading_offset_deg, alpha is zero and this rudder-induced component is zero; that is not total hull current load.
  • This report excludes: hull current force at oblique headings beyond the generic basis range, mooring-line stiffness, tug loads, bank effects, current-profile variation, propeller race, IMO/class compliance conclusions.
  • rudder-induced moored-current loads plus generic/reference OCIMF tanker-current review loads are reported for comparison
  • hull-current terms use report-specific coefficients resolved through the approved off-repo workbook route, not ship-specific SIROCCO current-coefficient curves or certified coefficients
  • bank effect, tug loads, mooring-line stiffness, current-profile variation, and propeller race are excluded
  • oblique-current extension is a first-cut visualization using alpha = rudder angle - heading offset
  • not a validated oblique-current hull/rudder interaction model
  • Cr=1.0 is a neutral no-propeller-rotation correction value
  • no class compliance conclusion

Method & Provenance

V=kn*0.51444; F=beta*A_R*V^2*Cr; alpha=delta-psi; X_local=F*sin(alpha)^2; Y_local=F*sin(alpha)*cos(alpha); X_ship=X_local*cos(psi)-Y_local*sin(psi); Y_ship=X_local*sin(psi)+Y_local*cos(psi); N=Y_ship*yaw_lever
  • Heading/rudder effective-angle convention: α = δ − ψ.
  • Local-to-ship transform rotates local current-frame X/Y loads into ship-fixed CoG axes.
  • Current review equations: q=0.5·ρ·V²; Cxc, Cyc, Cxyc interpolated from OCIMF MEG4 (2018) [1] Annex A figures A9/A10/A11, loaded-tanker WD/T>6 family; Xc=q·A_f·Cxc; Yc=q·A_l·Cyc; Nc=q·A_l·LBP·Cxyc.
  • Rudder Model A (default) per Whicker & Fehlner [3]: F = β·A_R·V²·Cr·sin(α).
  • Rudder Model B (comparison) per Faltinsen [2] §6.5: F = 0.5·ρ·A_R·V²·Cn(α) with Cn(α) ≈ 2π·sin(α) and stall cap at ±28°.
  • Scope note: hull current loads are screening estimates using OCIMF tanker-class coefficients; not a validated whole-vessel current-load or oblique-current hull/rudder interaction model.

Appendix A — Rudder model comparison (Whicker-Fehlner vs thin-plate)

Side-by-side comparison of the two screening rudder models at the default case (V=3.08 kn, ψ=+5°, δ=+28°, α=+23°). The chart below sweeps rudder angle 0..+28° at fixed ψ=+5° and the selected current speed, comparing Model A and Model B transverse force Y_rudder.

QuantityModel A — Whicker-Fehlner [3]Model B — thin-plate (Faltinsen [2] §6.5)
Normal force F (N)26450141955
Longitudinal X_rudder (kN)844
Transverse Y_rudder (kN, port)25135
Yaw moment N_rudder (kN·m, +bow-to-port)340418267

Both models are screening-level; neither is a validated rudder hydrodynamic model. Differences at large angles (>20°) reflect each model's simplifying assumptions and the stall cap in Model B.

References

  1. OCIMF (2018). Mooring Equipment Guidelines, 4th edition (MEG4). Witherby Publishing Group, Livingston, UK. ISBN 978-1-85609-771-0. Annex A — Wind and Current Coefficients for VLCC; figures A9 (Cxc longitudinal), A10 (Cyc transverse), A11 (Cxyc yaw moment); loaded-tanker family, water-depth-to-draft regime applied per §A.1 conventions.
  2. Faltinsen, O. M. (1990). Sea Loads on Ships and Offshore Structures. Cambridge University Press, Cambridge Ocean Technology Series. §6.5 — Rudder forces (thin-plate small-angle approximation Cn(α) ≈ 2π·sin(α)).
  3. Whicker, L. F. and Fehlner, L. F. (1958). Free-stream characteristics of a family of low-aspect-ratio, all-movable control surfaces for application to ship design. David Taylor Model Basin Report 933. (Basis for the β·A_R·V²·Cr·sin(α) rudder normal-force formulation used as Model A.)

Project Documents

  1. [P1] B1528 SIROCCO — Vessel Geometry and Rudder Particulars (workspace-hub issue #2569 source-pack). Project-internal vessel particulars, rudder dimensions, and Whicker-Fehlner β/Cr inputs. (Proprietary; available on request.)
  2. [P2] digitalmodel (open-source). Report generator: b1528_sirocco_current_heading_rudder_report.py. Function run_b1528_current_heading_rudder_report interpolates Annex A figures from the OCIMF MEG4 workbook held off-repo at the licensed publisher path; calculation-time fail-closed if the workbook or citation route cannot be resolved.
  3. [P3] Approved revision plan: workspace-hub issue #2760 plan (dated 2026-05-20).
